item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's still Euclid, the -impetus suffix is just something the author CWW likes to do on their articles.

Impetus in Latin means "attack", so it signifies that the object is hostile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn’t this be tagged esoteric-class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always loved the oddity that is 066. It's a musical ball of yarn that turned hostile when it felt threatened by The Foundation. Great concept and interesting addendum to follow up with.
+1

item.title: Good
item.content:encoded:
Ew… this SCP is gross, malevolent, and abhorrent.
I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why we're not the Destroy Destroy Destroy Foundation; we made one of our most adorable, precious items into something creepy and violent. Makes you wonder if SCP-1048 is only evil because somebody got annoyed and kicked it or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know why I found this so funny… but it's a fucking ball of yarn!!

It's also very creative. Nice use of the 'Eric' trope. Upvoted +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Turns out I'm a fool and reverted a thing, whoops!
Quickly undone, though. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really wish pages like these had an "Eric" tag. I know there's a number of tales and skips that deal with him, so it'd be nice to be able to find more on him fairly easily.

item.title: Re: Eric?
item.content:encoded:
His owner, duh.

Makes it pretty bad now.

item.title: Eric?
item.content:encoded: Eric?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably cause they lost the ability to hear and a infinite migrane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do this to everyone I meet. No complaints yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just Imagine a Person Doing This.

A person egg rolls up to you and pulls out a phone and speaker. He starts playing the Second Symphony at Deafening Volume and Rolls away.

+1

item.title: Re: Is it ok If I make an SCP idea linking to this one?
item.content:encoded:
Ok! I will make sure my article will benefit this one.

I will upload the actual "Idea" on my ideas page Here.

item.title: Re: Is it ok If I make an SCP idea linking to this one?
item.content:encoded: The short answer is you can crosslink articles as you want. The long answer is that this was rewritten by Communism will win in 2011, so you might want to check with him first, but his permission isn't strictly required. Additionally this is a Series I article, rated over +300, and therefore is going to under more scrutiny as a crosslink than newer or less popular articles. Which isn't to say not to crosslink it, but rather to say that the link really needs to benefit both articles.

item.title: Is it ok If I make an SCP idea linking to this one?
item.content:encoded: I have an SCP idea I plan to upload onto the sandbox. I want to link to this SCP somehow. is it ok if I do so?

item.title: Re: Second Image
item.content:encoded: Licensing team is going to reach out to the creator come monday and get a more concrete answer.

item.title: Re: Second Image
item.content:encoded: Do you know if the permission is given just for the SCP Wiki or is it released under Creative Commons? Can other SCP artists use the likeness of the SCP to the full extent of the CC license?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I first saw this little toy-like thing on Containment Breach actually, it was on Site19's Cafeteria.

So the article's really nice and original. loved it. Simple and that.

+Upvoted.

item.title: Second Image
item.content:encoded: The second image has been returned, as the original photographer/artist, Jacey Boggs, of www.insubordiknit.com has given us permission to use the image.

item.title: Re: Image Team
item.content:encoded: That's credited to Insubordiknit, which is owned by Jacey Boggs, who is the person credited with the eyeball yarn in the first place. Ms Boggs has already been contacted, and we're waiting for her reply.

item.title: Re: Image Team
item.content:encoded:
I was trying to find a better image for 066 by googling "yarn with eyeballs" and I came across this: http://www.instructables.com/community/Eyeball-Yarn/

This isn't the original source, but it'd probably help in finding and contacting the author.

item.title: Re: NEW IMAGES
item.content:encoded: That's what "(detail)" means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope the copyright stuff is resolved and we can keep using the older images.
Sigh.

item.title: Re: NEW IMAGES
item.content:encoded: Might want to tweak the caption for the 2nd image a bit, considering that one eye is visible. Maybe mention it's a close-up view?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[BEGIN LOG]
Dr. Bells: So, does this SCP have any incidents?
[paper rustling]
Dr. Bells: Umm, You there?
[eerie silence]
Dr. Bells: Ahem, I'd guess Dr. ████ is in the breakroom for now…
[Footsteps of Dr. ████ get closer to Dr. Bells]
Dr. ████: HAVEN'T YOU HEARD?! THEY ARE NEW IMAGES OF 066! YAY!
[END LOG]

item.title: NEW IMAGES
item.content:encoded:
I've found replacement images for this article.

The second one honestly doesn't hold a candle to the one that was originally there, and even the first one used to be better, so please feel free to message me if you know of a Creative Commons-compliant image that could be better.

Attributions are in my image attribution post on the first page.

item.title: Re: Image Team
item.content:encoded: I'll have to edit this article when I get back home. I really hope that image can stay in use, if only for the fanart this piece has gotten.

item.title: Image Team
item.content:encoded: SCP-066's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact me or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: Re: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: nevermind….

item.title: Re: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
This is probably SCP-066 messed up

pagename|VHC2.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This probably one of my favorite SCPs ever.

The concept is truly amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would you like to know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would love to hear more about SCP-066.

item.title: Re: Eric
item.content:encoded: SCP-683 and SCP-1248.

item.title: Re: Eric
item.content:encoded: There are other Eric SCPs? I can only find 168 after a short search, but are there other ones?

item.title: Re: Eric
item.content:encoded:
I personally love the headcanon set forth in that tale, but that's what headcanon is for.

Part of me thinks that the Eric in that universe somehow evaded the Foundation's notice for what he is even while joining them as a regular guy and rising in the ranks, simply because I don't know whether it's more realistic for them to completely miss his true nature or to appoint a known reality bender as an O5. Alternatively, maybe he lost his ability when he got older, though knowing the Foundation, that wouldn't matter.

item.title: Re: Eric
item.content:encoded: Seconding Voct - the Foundation has a habit of showing up after the events have already occurred, they may find documents and artifacts at the scene of recovery, but they very rarely find the reality bender/creature/whatever responsible for their creation.

item.title: Re: Eric
item.content:encoded:
I think the Foundation should never find Eric, ever. They only find artifacts connected with him.

The Foundation shows up after everything is finished and collects the leftover scraps.

item.title: Re: Eric
item.content:encoded: There's at least one.

item.title: Eric
item.content:encoded: Are there any Tales of Eric there should be since he is apprantly connected to this and other scps

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "Eric" set of articles makes me think that his mom or dad must have been anomalous as fuck, and making/influencing all his belongings. Leaning towards his mom for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like to hear beethoven at 140db ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I find this thing to be… Oddly adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing creeps me the hell out. +1

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
The first image was posted by Craftster user 'lapoli' here.

The second image was posted by Jacey Boggs here.

First image source is here under this license, and the second image source is here under this license. Both images were modified for this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good article, though the description is a bit of a bore. Nice pictures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, I love you Mr. Fuzzy

item.title: Re: Not clear that it's a toy
item.content:encoded:
I generally consider the tags to be "out of character" — that is, they can be outside of or different than what is strictly defined in the article. Thus, if I know something to be true about one of my SCPs, that information will be reflected in the tags without necessarily making it into the article.

On the whole, it's not particularly necessary for people to know that it's a toy for them to enjoy it. My primary justification for this is the rating.

item.title: Not clear that it's a toy
item.content:encoded:
I adore this. That being said, the current article doesn't really lead to a substantial amount of information to make the reader think it's a toy. What I was thinking after the article was that it was kind until it was attacked with the cutting, is now angry and wants to escape, and I guessed that maybe Eric was the creator of the yarn. As a newbie I didn't know the contents of the original and I think to other people who are reading this raw as well they won't know it was meant to be a toy.

Maybe like Dimitri says, the source may help. Maybe like a quick note on how it was found from a toy-related place might be all it needs for everything else to fall into place and make sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, no part of the article describes how the object was discovered. I guess that's not really important, though.
yay! Congrats scanny

item.title: THE PAIN!
item.content:encoded: I just realized that I neglected to add a space a between the words "instructed" and "to". I myself have just broken a grammatical rule. I shall now drink a glass of Sugar Water as punishment.

item.title: Grammatical mistake and general comment.
item.content:encoded:
On April 18, 2008, D-066-4437 to use a pair of scissors to remove a portion of SCP-066 for testing.

There should be a command issued here, such as "On April 18, 2008, D-066-4437 was instructedto use a pair of scissors to remove a portion of SCP-066 for testing."

Aside from that mistake; I found this rather interesting…..and amusing.

Imagine you're the agent who has to check for damages: You walk in, examine the wall, and stop to listen as you hear a sequence of six soft, chiming, notes. You dismiss the latter and continue to examine the wall and think nothing of the quietness that had been procured by the toy. Meanwhile, (Roughly 200 feet away.) A researcher is unable to focus on their work, due to the fact that Beethoven’s second symphony is blasting at an uncomfortable volume.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, ok. Wow, that just seems to make it all the more unnatural, which is the point, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it was a real cat, but it's not. "…appeared to be…'

Still if the SCP had been an actual cat, that would be a good call.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might just be nitpicking, but under the description of the kitten form, it's stated to be both calico and neutered. Calico cats and almost universally female, while the term "neutered" refers to a fixed male. If the kitten is male and calico, a note should probably be made. Otherwise, the term "spayed" would fit a fixed female better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the quirky meaninglessness of this SCP. Good work reviving a dying SCP. Yay, I'm not just a grumpy downvoter! Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took him by surprise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this story. I'm just surprised that the researcher didn't say his name was Eric.
